content.problemStatement:
To convert a number from decimal to binary:
- check the remainder of the number when divided by 2, and add it to front of the binary number
- divide the number by 2
- repeat until the number is 0

Using these steps, let's convert 203 to binary.
- remainder is 1. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 1.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 101.
- remainder is 1. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 11.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 50.
- remainder is 0. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 25.
- remainder is 1. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 1011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 12.
- remainder is 0. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 01011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 6.
- remainder is 0. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 001011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 3.
- remainder is 1. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 1001011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 1.
- remainder is 1. After adding it to the front of the binary number, the binary number becomes 11001011.
- dividing the number by 2, it becomes 0.

Using these steps convert $23$ from decimal number system to binary number system.
constraints:
- $1 \leq T \leq 1000$
- $2 \leq N \leq 10^5$
- $1 \leq A_i \leq 10^9$
- The sum of $N$ across all test cases does not exceed $10^5$
content.inputFormat:
- The first line of input will contain a single integer $T$, denoting the number of test cases. The description of the test cases follows.
- The first line of each test case contains a single integer $N$, denoting the length of array $A$.
- The second line of each test case contains $N$ space-separated integers $A_1, A_2, \ldots, A_N$ — denoting the array $A$.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, output on a new line the maximum value of $A_1+A_N$ you can get after several right rotations.
